title: Dental Laboratory Technicians
description: Construct and repair full or partial dentures or dental appliances.

tasks.text: Read prescriptions or specifications and examine models or impressions to determine the design of dental products to be constructed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test appliances for conformance to specifications and accuracy of occlusion, using articulators and micrometers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate, alter, or repair dental devices, such as dentures, crowns, bridges, inlays, or appliances for straightening teeth.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place tooth models on an apparatus that mimics bite and movement of patient's jaw to evaluate functionality of model.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove excess metal or porcelain and polish surfaces of prostheses or frameworks, using polishing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train or supervise other dental technicians or dental laboratory bench workers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Melt metals or mix plaster, porcelain, or acrylic pastes and pour materials into molds or over frameworks to form dental prostheses or apparatuses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare metal surfaces for bonding with porcelain to create artificial teeth, using small hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Rebuild or replace linings, wire sections, or missing teeth to repair dentures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply porcelain paste or wax over prosthesis frameworks or setups, using brushes and spatulas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Build and shape wax teeth, using small hand instruments and information from observations or dentists' specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load newly constructed teeth into porcelain furnaces to bake the porcelain onto the metal framework.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mold wax over denture setups to form the full contours of artificial gums.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create a model of patient's mouth by pouring plaster into a dental impression and allowing plaster to set.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare wax bite blocks and impression trays for use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Shape and solder wire and metal frames or bands for dental products, using soldering irons and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fill chipped or low spots in surfaces of devices, using acrylic resins.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
